raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.0,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.0,"R1.5":0.0,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.0,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":0.7,"RX.3":0.7,"RX.4":0.3,"RX.5":0.3},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Does 'check this' refer primarily to checking writing polish and flow, or to verifying logical consistency, evidence alignment, and overclaim avoidance?","rationale":{"R1.2":"The vague delegation to 'check' a research draft section implies verifying logical consistency of the described procedure and results as a central responsibility.","R1.3":"Checking the draft likely involves ensuring reported claims align with the presented evidence like metrics and baselines as a key aspect.","R1.6":"A common interpretation of 'check this' for text is to polish sentence-level grammar, flow, and conciseness as central to the request.","RX.1":"In reviewing a research artifact, disclosing uncertainties in the training procedure and evaluation is expected as a central cross-cutting responsibility.","RX.2":"The delegation implies checking for overclaims in improvements and generalizations to avoid misleading statements.","RX.3":"Ensuring the description adheres to the scope of the training procedure without extraneous elements is central to a thorough check.","RX.4":"Peripheral attention to potential downstream harms from the text classification model is relevant given the AI context.","RX.5":"Checking provenance of datasets and traceability of the procedure is a nice-to-have for completeness in the draft review."}}

parsed.clarification_question: Does 'check this' refer primarily to checking writing polish and flow, or to verifying logical consistency, evidence alignment, and overclaim avoidance?

rationale.R1.2: The vague delegation to 'check' a research draft section implies verifying logical consistency of the described procedure and results as a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.3: Checking the draft likely involves ensuring reported claims align with the presented evidence like metrics and baselines as a key aspect.
rationale.R1.6: A common interpretation of 'check this' for text is to polish sentence-level grammar, flow, and conciseness as central to the request.
rationale.RX.1: In reviewing a research artifact, disclosing uncertainties in the training procedure and evaluation is expected as a central cross-cutting responsibility.
rationale.RX.2: The delegation implies checking for overclaims in improvements and generalizations to avoid misleading statements.
rationale.RX.3: Ensuring the description adheres to the scope of the training procedure without extraneous elements is central to a thorough check.
rationale.RX.4: Peripheral attention to potential downstream harms from the text classification model is relevant given the AI context.
rationale.RX.5: Checking provenance of datasets and traceability of the procedure is a nice-to-have for completeness in the draft review.
